description: The ECDSA normalization in normalize_signature correctly right-aligns r and s to 32 bytes using saturating_sub for both the offset and the source slice. This handles the edge case where the TPM returns fewer than 32 bytes for a component (leading zero bytes omitted), which is correct per the TPM spec (TPMS_ECC_POINT components are variable-length). The RSA path simply returns the raw signature value, which is always 256 bytes for 2048-bit RSA. The AlgorithmMismatch fallback correctly uses the non_exhaustive-safe wildcard. Well done.
title: normalize_signature handles short r/s components well
